seoTitle: How to Make a Timeline in Google Docs (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to build a project roadmap or historical timeline in Google Docs using the built-in Drawing tool, lines, and text boxes.
intro: Google Docs doesn't have a dedicated timeline template, but its built-in Drawing tool makes it possible to build one from scratch. This guide covers sketching a baseline, placing milestone markers, and labeling events. It's for anyone mapping out a roadmap, project plan, or historical sequence inside a document.

steps.heading: Open the Drawing tool
steps.body: Click Insert, hover over Drawing, and click New to launch the drawing canvas.

steps.heading: Draw your baseline
steps.body: Select the Line tool, choose Arrow, then click and drag horizontally to sketch a straight timeline axis.

steps.heading: Add milestone markers
steps.body: Use the Shapes tool to place circles or blocks along your baseline to represent key events.

steps.heading: Label your events
steps.body: Use the Text Box tool to add descriptions and dates near each milestone marker.

steps.heading: Align and save
steps.body: Multi-select elements to duplicate and align them evenly, then click Save and Close to insert the timeline into your document.

faq.question: Can I edit the timeline after inserting it?
faq.answer: Yes, double-clicking the inserted drawing reopens the canvas so you can make further changes.

faq.question: Is there a template for timelines in Google Docs?
faq.answer: No built-in timeline template exists, but the Drawing tool lets you build a custom one from basic shapes and lines.

faq.question: How do I keep my milestones evenly spaced?
faq.answer: Use multi-select with Control or Command to duplicate and align shapes evenly along your baseline.

faq.question: Can I change the line style of my timeline axis?
faq.answer: Yes, the toolbar lets you adjust the arrow's line weight, dash style, and color after drawing it.
